name: A T Still University of Health Sciences
oedb_degree_levels: Doctoral Degree, Masters Degree, Post-Bachelors Certificate, Post-Masters Certificate
oedb_description: A.T. Still University opened the world's first osteopathic medical school in 1892. The college trains physicians and healthcare administrators who specialize in a whole-person approach to medicine. ATSU enrolls about 3,000 students annually and operates campuses in Missouri and Arizona. Through a virtual campus, online learners earn one of several doctoral programs in health education, health science, and administration. The doctor of health administration prepares students for effective industry leadership. The three-year degree focuses on topics such as healthcare law, health policy, medical care economics, and health information systems. The Ed.D. in health professions immerses students in experience-based learning designed for practicing clinicians, healthcare leaders, educators, and similar professionals. Ed.D. coursework covers topics such as educational program evaluation, students assessment, and transformative leadership and ethics. A third program, the doctor of health sciences, develops effective managers who excel in organizational leadership, evidence-based standards, and applying research to professional practice.

oedb_description: Alexandria Technical & Community College in Alexandria, Minnesota offers a paralegal online associate degree that prepares students to perform legal work under an attorney’s direct supervision. This program provides students with extensive training in preparing and handling legal documents and legal correspondence, legal ethics, and trial preparation. Coursework covers a variety of topics, including criminal procedures, family law, estates and probate, and bankruptcy. Students in the program learn about relevant computer applications, such as how to use databases, word processing software, spreadsheets, and presentation software. Advanced paralegal courses are available to students with an overall GPA of 2.5 or higher. Graduates can pursue work as paralegals, legal assistants, bankruptcy analysts, title company closers, credit investigators, property specialists, or senior court clerks. The college also offers job searching resources to distance learners. Transfer students are widely accepted at Alexandria Technical & Community College; Minnesota Transfer Curriculum classes transfer over immediately, while other classes are evaluated on an individual basis.

oedb_description: Allen College in Waterloo, Iowa, offers fully online and hybrid programs geared toward distance learners and working professionals, including one of the nation’s best online RN to BSN programs. The 124-credit RN to BSN program provides top-quality instruction at a reasonable price and can be completed full-time or part-time. Coursework is delivered through Blackboard and covers topics such as professional writing, nursing informatics, and nursing leadership. The curricula includes 30 BSN credits and general education requirements, and transfer credits are accepted. Allen College maintains partnerships with regional institutions, including Northeast Iowa Community College, to streamline credit transfers. Applicants must have at least a 2.5 GPA.

oedb_description: The District of Columbia’s oldest accredited business school, American University's school of business offers an MBA online degree program for domestic and global professionals. Students can choose one of six predesigned concentrations or create their own self-designed degree track. Predesigned options include finance, marketing, international business, consulting, business analytics, and cybersecurity. With a comprehensive curriculum, students take classes in forensic accounting, investment analysis, portfolio management, database and big data, and predictive analytics. Students must participate in two three- or four-day "business in capitals" immersions, where they have the opportunity to network with classmates, professors, and other business professionals. Individuals looking for an online MBA with no GMAT requirements will be happy to learn that there are no standardized test requirements for admission into American University. Students can begin this practice-based program in October, January, April, or July.

oedb_description: Andrews University is an Adventist college located in Berrien Springs, Michigan. Students at all levels receive instruction that blends scholarship and practical Christianity. Andrews' e-learning platform enables learners to earn an online doctorate in healthcare, leadership, or education. The 64-credit doctor of science in physical therapy prepares learners for Certified Manual Physical Therapist credentials, and offers two specializations: women's health or orthopaedic manual therapy. Alternatively, the online doctor of physical therapy's 35- to 38-credit curriculum provides advanced training to practicing physical therapists. Mid-career leaders can optimize their skills and advance credentials with either the 54-credit doctor of leadership, which culminates in a project, or 60-credit Ph.D. in leadership, which requires a dissertation. Educators seeking to advance into leadership roles acquire the necessary training when they complete either the Ed.D. or Ph.D. in higher education administration. Students who want to maintain a focus on classroom instruction enroll in either the Ed.D. or Ph.D. in curriculum and instruction.

oedb_description: Located in Wilmore, Kentucky, Asbury University’s 124-credit BS in elementary education offers students both general education courses and 82-credit hours immersed in major-related studies. In addition to exposure to education theory, students complete hands-on student teaching and a guided inquiry clinical. The program also includes a three-course sequence in biblical studies and a 12-credit sequence of interdisciplinary liberal arts courses. Applicants need a high school diploma or its equivalent and must submit official transcripts. Letters of reference are also required. The admissions office encourages applicants to contact one of their advisors prior to beginning the application process. Asbury allows students to transfer up to 75 credits from other institutions, and all students pay the same tuition rate irrespective of state residency.

oedb_description: Founded in 1878, Ashland University has a long history of providing students with a strong liberal arts education. The online bachelor’s degree in criminal justice instructs students in both theory and practical skills. Students take courses in criminal profiling, gangs and cults, ethics, and the role of police in modern society. Classes are offered in eight-week cycles. Students who already work in law enforcement or corrections in the State of Ohio may be eligible for work-related credits. Incoming freshmen should have a 2.5 GPA or above and transfer students should have grades of "C" or above. Transfer students are welcome at Ashland. The school hosts events called “Transfer Thursdays” where prospective students can meet with admissions and do a full transcript review. Ashland also offers online and on-campus tours for potential students.

oedb_description: Atlanta Technical College offers an online associate degree in paralegal studies that prepares students for successful paralegal careers in a variety of settings. Students learn to conduct legal research, deliver legal arguments, produce legal documents, and use appropriate and proper legal terminology during written and verbal communication. The 69-credit hour curriculum covers a wide range of topics, including computer literacy, law office management, law and ethics, legal and policy issues in health care, and constitutional law. The program also requires students to complete an internship as part of their graduation requirements. The purpose of the internship is to provide students with hands-on experience, enabling them to apply the information they learned in their courses and see how these skills translate into the field. The program length is six semesters, which is typically completed within two years by full-time students. To graduate, students must maintain a minimum 2.0 GPA.

oedb_description: Babson College offers an online MBA degree that has a few on-site components. The program commences with a week-long startup residency, where students participate in simulations and networking events and take a class in entrepreneurship and leadership. Throughout the duration of the program, Babson offers signature learning experiences, where students have the opportunity to apply their newfound knowledge to complex problems. Notable classes in the curriculum include mergers and acquisitions, brand management, entrepreneurial finance, global strategic management, global marketing strategy, and product management. Online learners participate in synchronous, real-time webinars and conferences as well as asynchronous discussion boards. The program culminates with a final corporate entrepreneurship capstone project, in which students form teams to develop a business plan. Students can choose a concentration in business analytics, entrepreneurship, finance, global management, or marketing. Incoming students have an average of 10 years of professional work experience.

oedb_description: Baker University was founded in 1858 as the first university in Kansas. Today, Baker enrolls over 3,200 students annually. Baker’s online bachelor of business administration in management consists of 42 major credits, with courses including business analytics, leveraging technology in decision making, and best practices in management and leadership. Students also take on coursework in their area of concentration, choosing from either human resources, finance, or healthcare administration. All distance learners enjoy the same affordable tuition rates, priced per-credit based on the level of the class. Over 98% of Baker students receive financial aid in the form of scholarships and grants. The university also allows students to earn college credit from previous academic work, professional training, and life experience via assessment.

oedb_description: Students who want to earn a degree at one of the accounting programs online will be interested to learn the details of the bachelor’s of science in business-accounting online program at the American Women’s College at Bay Path University in Longmeadow, Mass. Bay Path is a private university with a history of supporting female students. The accounting program provides instruction in the fundamentals of accounting theory, like auditing, business ethics/laws, and financial accounting. Available courses include federal income tax concepts, financial reporting, and contemporary issues in accounting. Bay Path also requires students to take courses related to career development, like strategies for career growth and leadership in practice. Courses last six weeks, and there are six sessions each year. The accelerated format makes it possible to earn the BS in one to three-and-a-half years. Up to 90 credits can be transferred into the program.

oedb_description: Located in Saint Paul, Minnesota, Bethel University offers 35 online academic pathways, including a bachelor of science in business management . Designed for working professionals, this degree program consists of 51 credits in the major, including core classes like foundations of human resource management and corporate finance. Advanced coursework differs depending on whether a student chooses the general management or global business concentration. All degree candidates must complete a business capstone experience in which they propose a solution to pertinent client needs within an organization. Most students earn their diploma in 16 to 21 months. Applicants must hold have at least 60 prior college credits in order to be eligible for Bethel’s online business degree program. Tuition is priced at the same per-credit rate for all distance learners.

oedb_description: Buena Vista University in Storm Lake, Iowa offers an online bachelor’s degree in criminology and criminal justice . Graduates from this program seek jobs in the judicial system, criminal justice system, and in law enforcement. Classes in criminal justice include criminal justice systems, modern social problems, judicial process, and criminological theory. Online classes start every eight weeks. BVU accepts transfer students with an associate degree or 60 credit hours from an accredited school. Admission staff also consider CLEP exams and life and work experience for credits. BVU provides students with financial specialists that can help them calculate costs of tuition and choose which program is the best for their lifestyle. Applicants are encouraged to work with an advisor when going through the application process. Students will continue to work with an advisor throughout their schooling to plan a course of study to complete the program.

oedb_description: Bushnell University offers degree-completion programs online and on campus one night per week. The school features a 12-to-one student-to-faculty ratio and offers seven degrees, including a BS in business administration . Courses start every eight weeks, with six terms offered per year, and students can begin the program at the start of any term. Online students located near the campus in Eugene, Oregon can combine online coursework with face-to-face instruction. There are nine required courses in this major. Students also choose three additional classes from options such as marketing research; consumer behavior; and branding, advertising, and promotion. Students interested in earning this marketing bachelor's degree online should submit their application and college transcripts. High school transcripts are also required of any student who has earned less than 12 college credits. Distance learners with significant work and life experiences may be able to earn up to 30 credits through the school’s prior learning assessment program. While admission to BU typically requires a GPA of at least 2.0, individuals with lower grades may be accepted through conditional admission.

oedb_description: While earning CBU’s online graphic design degree, students develop individual points of view and learn industry-standard design techniques. The BA in graphic design and digital media incorporates disciplines such as drawing, compositing, photography, presentation, lighting, and color theory. Students also learn advanced techniques in applications such as Adobe Dreamweaver, Illustrator, Photoshop, and InDesign. Along with general education requirements, the program requires 48 major credits. Applicants should hold a high school diploma or its equivalent. While first-time freshman applicants are welcome, transfer applicants with 24 or more college credits and a GPA of at least 2.0 are preferred. All students pay the same tuition rate, regardless of state residency.

oedb_description: Calumet College of Saint Joseph in Whiting, Indiana offers an online criminal justice associate program for students interested in pursuing work as a paralegal. The program has concentrations in probation, parole, and courts; forensic science; and pre-law or professional studies. Students interested in a paralegal career should enroll in the pre-law or professional studies concentration. Curriculum covers a variety of topics, such as deviant behavior, interviewing techniques, criminal law, criminal procedure, and criminology. To graduate from this program, students must complete an internship and a senior seminar in criminal justice. The internship lets students see first-hand what it is like to work in local, state, and federal law enforcement and criminal justice agencies. The senior seminar is a capstone course where students create a presentation, highlighting what they learned during the program. To be admitted, potential students must provide their high school transcripts and ACT or SAT scores.

oedb_description: Campbell University in Buies Creek, N.C., offers an accounting degree online. Campbell is a private university with an enrollment of more than 6,000 undergraduate and graduate students. The BBA in accounting offers the curriculum completely online, with no in-person requirements, and eight-week accelerated semesters allow students greater flexibility and the ability to graduate more quickly. The program prepares students for work as CPAs in environments including government, corporations, and nonprofit groups. The degree provides a broad background in general principles of business, and is considered especially good preparation for students who want to try entrepreneurship. The 124-credit bachelor’s of business administration requires 42 general education credits and 78 within the accounting concentration, and students must maintain a minimum 2.0 GPA to graduate. Transfer students may receive credit for up to 64 semester hours from a regionally-accredited institution. Accounting courses at Campbell vary, from philosophy of business to management information systems to corporate finance.

oedb_description: Capitol Technology University is the premier STEM college in Washington, D.C. Located in Laurel, Maryland, Capitol enables skilled tech professionals to work in governmental agencies and private sector supply chains in the D.C. metro area. Capitol's 15 online Ph.D.s cover the school's specialities, including artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, critical infrastructure, and business analytics and data science. The 60-credit Ph.D. in human factors develops researchers and practitioners adept in the design of products, equipment, and systems that provide humans with a safe and enjoyable user experience. The online 60-credit Ph.D. in aviation enrolls industry professionals equipped to play a central role in major technological innovation. A related program, the 60-credit Ph.D. in unmanned systems applications, trains researchers, leaders, and educators to contribute original research to the field. Most online programs require a commitment of 2-3 years, research, and a dissertation. Capitol's location gives learners access to one of the nation's top 20 strongest job markets.

oedb_description: Carnegie Mellon offers a part-time online MBA designed for working professionals. The curriculum of this program focuses on strategic analytics and leadership development, and students receive leadership and communication coaching throughout the duration of the program. Classes include managerial economics, managing networks and organizations, corporate strategy, and interpersonal communication. Students also complete a capstone project in their second year of study. Online students participate in both synchronous, interactive sessions and asynchronous, self-paced coursework. Distance learners also attend "access weekends," which are held every two months for a total of six weekends each year; these provide ample opportunities for networking. Students typically earn their degrees in three years. This program’s low one-to-five student-to-teacher ratio sets it apart from many other schools and allows for increased attention from faculty. Online learners also receive recruiting support through a partnership with the Accelerate Leadership Center. Admitted students begin classes in September.

oedb_description: Located in western Ohio, Cedarville University offers an online MBA degree for Christian professionals. Designed with the belief that professional work is a platform for ministry, this program offers graduate students three options for study: a generalist MBA, a concentration in operations management, or a concentration in healthcare administration. Core courses include executive financial management, information technology for competitive advantage, managerial data analytics, and organizational design and behavior. Students must also take a biblical and theological foundation course. Course activities include projects, simulations, and case studies. Applicants must hold a bachelor's degree and make a profession of faith. Online coursework is delivered through Moodle, Hoonuit, and Google Hangouts. Although an undergraduate degree in business is not required, students must complete certain prerequisites, including financial and managerial accounting, fundamentals of economics, and business statistics. Admitted students must participate in an online orientation.

oedb_description: Chaminade offers two online degrees in education that qualify graduates to work as teachers at any grade level. Students may pursue either the BS in elementary education (K-6) or the BA in secondary education (grades 6-12). Either of these degrees lead to licensure in the state, and most states accept a Hawaii teaching license. Both programs also consist of 120 credits that include general core, education core, elective courses, and on-site student teaching. Applicants need a high school diploma with a cumulative GPA of at least 2.75, transcripts, ACT or SAT scores of 19 or 920-1000, respectively, and personal statement. Transfer applicants need a cumulative college GPA of 2.0, and all students pay the same tuition rate irrespective of state residency.

oedb_description: Champlain College in Burlington, Vt., offers distance learners the opportunity to earn an accredited online accounting degree . Students must complete 120 credits in order to graduate from the program -- 57 in general education and 63 in accounting. The bachelor’s of science in accounting degree is designed for busy working adults, with most courses presented in accelerated seven-week sessions. Classes offered include managing behavior in organizations, cost accounting, and decision making through critical thinking. The program strives to provide students with a solid understanding of the broad theories of accounting, as well as practical skills needed to succeed in the field today. Online learners may also earn certificates in accounting, advanced accounting, or cost accounting as they progress through the curriculum. As students approach graduation, they take an accounting capstone course, in which they reflect on their time in the program, identify current problems or questions in the field of accounting, and research solutions.

oedb_description: Charleston Southern University offers a BT in computer science for students who hold associate degrees in approved technical fields. This online bachelor's in computer science degree consists mostly of upper level classes that prepare students for a successful career in technology. Since each student comes from a different background, faculty members work with each individual to create a program that fits their schedule and education. Transfer students beginning with the maximum of 89 hours often graduate within two years. Associate degrees that fit best with this program include engineering technology, computer technology, and telecommunications management. Prior to enrollment, prospective students must complete a college math course and an in-person programming class in C++ or Visual Basic. Students need a minimum of 125 credit hours to graduate.

oedb_description: Founded in 1869 in Pittsburgh, Chatham offers an online RN to BSN program tailored to working professionals with busy schedules. The program is accredited by the Commission on Collegiate Nursing Education and is available in an accelerated format, which students can complete within a year. Coursework covers topics including nursing communication and quality improvement, women’s health nursing, and health policy and finance. The curriculum emphasizes critical thinking skills and the liberal arts. Students complete three self-directed practice exercises and may pursue a minor in business or psychology. Applicants must have at least a 2.0 GPA, and students must maintain a 2.0 GPA in order to graduate. Transfer students may bring up to 90 of the 120 required credits.

The bachelor of science in applied computing from CityU gives students hands-on experience in tech. Distance learners develop skills in Python, information security, and systems analysis and design. The program requires a capstone project at the end where students demonstrate the knowledge they learned through examinations, written work, and oral presentations. CityU maintains a year-round schedule and a rolling admissions policy. Applicants must show proof of a cumulative 2.0 GPA. This degree requires 180 credit hours for students to graduate. Students may transfer a maximum of 135 transfer credits towards this degree. Prior learning assessments give students with practical the chance to apply their knowledge for school credit. Students coming from out-of-state do not pay higher tuition.

oedb_description: One of the best online MBA programs in the country, Clarkson University offers graduate students a business education on a part-time or full-time basis. With a comprehensive curriculum of 11 core courses and five electives, students in this 48-credit program are prepared to lead organizations across a variety of industries. Classes include decision analysis and supply chain modeling, business information systems, corporate ethical and social responsibility, and managerial economics. Students also complete a leadership and organizational behavior class, which is offered as an on-site intensive weekend course. The program ends with a capstone project in strategic management. To graduate, MBA students without professional business experience must complete 400 hours of employment in a business setting. Online learners can choose between asynchronous and synchronous learning options. Admitted students can begin at the start of any term.

oedb_description: Cleary University focuses on business degrees, offering associate, bachelor’s, and master’s programs in business administration. Its BBA in marketing is a 120-credit, online marketing degree that can be completed by full-time students in four years. The program includes courses in business foundations, communications, management, and marketing, and distance learners conclude the program by completing a professional project. Students can utilize elective credits to take career development courses. Resume assistance, mock interviews, and career search tips are also available to distance learners. New online students can complete an online orientation to learn about additional services, such as academic support and technology assistance. The orientation also involves a required one-credit course that familiarizes students with the library, academic policies, and Moodle learning management system. All undergraduate students are charged the same tuition, regardless of their residency. Additionally, the university’s block pricing allows students to take between 12 and 18 credits during the semester for the same tuition.

oedb_description: Founded in 1889, Clemson University offers innovative online programs, including a respected RN to BSN program , which is accredited by the Commission on Collegiate Nursing Education. The affordable program is one of only 35 programs in the nation to be recognized as a Center of Excellence in Nursing Education by the National League of Nursing since 2004. Students may apply up to six credits toward a master’s degree in nursing. The curriculum covers topics such as the nursing care of children, mental health nursing, and leadership and management in nursing. Applicants must have at least a 3.0 GPA, and students must maintain a cumulative GPA of at least 2.5 in all nursing coursework.

oedb_description: Colorado State University’s BS in marketing is an online degree that can be customized by adding a 15-credit specialization. More than 15 specializations are available, including business administration, data management and analysis, project management, and public relations. The 120-credit online bachelor's degree in marketing consists of courses such as digital marketing, international and multicultural marketing, and product and brand management. Students also complete a marketing capstone course where they analyze a company and design long- and short-term marketing plans. CSU guarantees that tuition will never increase while a student is enrolled, and there are no student fees. Online courses are offered asynchronously throughout the year, and distance learners can enroll before any term. Applicants to bachelor’s programs should have at least 12 transferable college credits with a minimum GPA of 2.3. Students who do not meet this minimum can apply for provisional admission. The university accepts transfer credit, credit earned through exams, and credit earned through prior learning assessments.

oedb_description: Community Care College in Tulsa, Oklahoma offers students a paralegal associate degree that is 100% online. Students can access their instructors by phone, text, email, chat, Skype, and in-person. The curriculum for this program is created and taught by a licensed attorney who graduated from Harvard Law School. Coursework encompasses a variety of topics, including real estate law, family law, paralegal ethics, American government, and legal writing and research. The program is accelerated and can be completed in less than a year. Students learn to research laws, regulations, and legal articles; organize legal documents; use electronic filing systems; prepare cases and evidence for attorneys to review; help lawyers prepare for trials by writing summaries and reports; collect affidavits and statements to be used in court; and conduct client interviews. Graduates can pursue employment as paralegals and legal assistants who help and support lawyers.

oedb_description: Founded as a Christian high school in the 1920s, Concordia University Texas maintains strong ties to the Lutheran church. The school offers a BA in computer science for working students looking to pursue a career in software design or computer programming. Students benefit from the school’s location in Austin, a technology hub with ample internship opportunities. This online computer science degree requires students to complete a minimum of 128 credits to graduate. CTX boasts a strong Christian foundation and requires all students to pass religion-based courses. Transfer students need a minimum cumulative 2.0 GPA if transferring between 12 and 83 credits or a 2.5 GPA if transferring less than 12 credits. Out-of-state students pay the same as Texas residents. The Texas Transfer Scholarship offers students $2,000 in financial aid if they graduate with an associate degree in the state or qualify for the Texas Core Complete program.

oedb_description: Students in Concordia University-Saint Paul’s BA in marketing program learn about integrated promotional strategies, new media and social media literacy, and marketing analysis. Graduates are prepared to become effective marketing and project managers who can analyze the critical factors that drive business. This 120-credit degree includes 40 credits in the marketing major, and the entire program can be completed online. Online courses are seven weeks long and are taught asynchronously. Students pursuing an online marketing degree at Concordia receive a tuition discount relative to their on-campus peers and are guaranteed that tuition will not increase while they are enrolled. Applicants should have a minimum GPA of 2.0, and individuals who have earned less than 20 college credits need to submit their high school transcripts. Students without college experience should also submit their standardized test scores.

oedb_description: Closely tied to the Lutheran church, Concordia University has been providing a Christian education for more than 125 years. Concordia is based out of Mequon, Wisconsin and has locations throughout Wisconsin and Michigan. The online bachelor’s degree in management of criminal justice is designed for students who wish to take on leadership roles in law enforcement agencies. The 128 credit program includes coursework in procedural criminal law and criminal investigation. The faculty at Concordia are experts in their field and provide a real-world learning environment for students. Concordia offers an online orientation that teaches new students how to use the program’s technology platform. Classes take 8-10 weeks to finish. Concordia accepts transfer students and allows them to apply up to 80 credits from an approved college. The university also considers real-world experience for credit and accepts military credit. A GPA of 2.0 is necessary to enter the major.

oedb_description: Corban offers one of the best online psychology degree programs in the country. Students learn the fundamentals of psychological theory. Located in Salem, Oregon, Corban helps students unite their career and their Christian faith. Students earning Corban’s bachelor’s in psychology study topics such as community agency counseling, juvenile delinquency, abnormal psychology, and family violence and dysfunction. Psychology students can pursue a concentration in family studies, which prepares them for careers in social work and ministry, or pre-counseling/clinical, which prepares students to enter a graduate counseling or psychology program. Applicants should demonstrate faith in Jesus Christ and a Christian lifestyle and should have strong writing abilities and transferable credit with at least a 2.0 GPA. Applicants should also have at least one year of work, college, or life experience after graduating high school.

oedb_description: Omaha, Nebraska-based Creighton University consistently ranks among the nation's best schools for online learning. The Jesuit school offers online doctoral programs that cover business, healthcare, and education. The 54-credit DBA offers a hybrid curriculum accredited by the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business. It blends classroom instruction and applied research to optimize students' managerial and leadership skills. Creighton manages a strong nursing program that culminates in the DNP. Faculty provide a practical curriculum that develops highly skilled and compassionate nurses. The hybrid curriculum offers multiple DNP tracks and the flexibility for working nurses to proceed at their own pace. The 143-credit doctor of pharmacy, also hybrid, prepares experts in patient data collection, pharmacy care planning, patient counseling, and medication therapy management and delivery. The 60-credit Ed.D. in interdisciplinary leadership offers an adaptable curriculum suitable for participants with eyes on senior positions in education, business, or other fields. All online students pay the same per-credit tuition regardless of residency.

oedb_description: Located in Madison, South Dakota, Dakota State University boasts a high employment rate for graduates, with a 94% employment rate for the latest class and a 100% employment rate in certain majors. DSU’s online bachelor’s degree in computer science gives students access to the newest technologies so they can stay ahead of their peers. Courses include assembly language, object-oriented design, and database management. Graduates often pursue careers as programmers, software engineers, or tech startup entrepreneurs. DSU schedules on-campus and online courses at the same time, so both student groups have the opportunity to collaborate with each other on projects. All computer science professors have a Ph.D. This program requires transfer students to have 24 credit hours or more. Applicants must hold a cumulative GPA of 2.0 or higher.

oedb_description: Dallas Baptist University offers a number of online certificates and degrees, all of which are accessible through the Blackboard learning management system. Undergraduate online programs include associate and bachelor’s degrees, including a BBA in marketing . This online marketing bachelor's degree requires the completion of 120 credit hours, featuring 33 business core credits and 30 marketing credits. DBU also offers a 120-credit BBS in marketing online. To apply, prospective first-year students must submit their application with an essay, high school transcripts, and standardized test scores. Transfer applicants must also submit their college transcripts. The university’s admission requirements include a minimum GPA of 2.5, and some programs may require minimum test scores. Students who do not meet these standards may be accepted probationally. DBU may also award course credit for corporate training and/or military experience. Financial aid, scholarships, and payment plans are available to distance learners, and all students pay the same per-credit undergraduate tuition.

oedb_description: The bachelor’s of business administration in marketing at Davenport University includes courses in both business and technology. This program can be completed online or on-campus and allows students to customize their major by choosing an optional specialization in digital marketing and advertising, value chain, or marketing technology. Each specialization consists of six courses. Graduates are prepared to manage information using digital technologies and develop strategies that leverage and integrate information into marketing plans. Davenport’s curriculum also lets marketing students earn various certifications by completing certain coursework; for example, students can earn HubSpot certifications in professional selling and digital marketing. This online bachelor's degree in marketing is delivered asynchronously through the Blackboard learning management system.

oedb_description: Located in Chicago’s business district, DePaul University serves students from all 50 states and 114 countries. The award-winning school has been ranked one of the best national universities by U.S. News & World Report and one of the best Midwestern colleges by the Princeton Review. DePaul’s online bachelor’s degree in computer science is a degree completion program for students who have already completed prior college coursework. Notable courses include software development, database systems, and applied computing. Seniors complete a capstone software project. Incoming students must provide proof of a minimum of 45 quarter hours or 30 semester hours of transfer credit, including one semester of C++ or Java. Applicants must also hold a cumulative 2.0 GPA. The school grants scholarships to students that demonstrate academic excellence, school involvement and leadership, and a dedication to serving others. In-state and out-of-state tuition for this online bachelor's in computer science degree are the same.

oedb_description: Students in the online BA in psychology program at DeSales learn to understand behavior and mental functions through careful examination of humans and animals. Graduates enter mental health professions, forensic-related careers, and graduate school. The 120-credit program includes classes in psychopathology, psychobiology, and research design and analysis. Online coursework may be asynchronous, synchronous, or a combination of the two. DeSales accepts up to 75 transfer credits and awards credit for College Board advanced placement exam scores, military training, and portfolio assessments. Students can test out of certain classes after demonstrating mastery of the subject matter by passing an exam and completing additional requirements, such as a presentation or paper.

oedb_description: Located in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, Drexel University offers an online education degree on a part-time basis as a degree completion program to students with prior college credit. Students complete the BS in education in as few as 30 months across 10 quarter terms. The degree provides graduates with PK-4 elementary teacher certification or PK-4 and special education certification. Students must earn a total of 180 quarter credits in order to graduate, including general education and major related courses and 18 quarter credits of student teaching equal to 190 real world hours. Applicants must already possess at least 24 college credits with a GPA of 2.5 or higher at a regionally accredited institution. The school will consider applicants with less than 24 college credits who have been out of high school for more than two years. Applicants must submit standardized test scores and official transcripts.

oedb_description: Drury University in Springfield, Missouri has a paralegal associate degree program that is fully online and teaches students the fundamentals of American law. The program is comprised of 21 credits of paralegal courses and 41 credits of general education courses. Classes cover many topics, including legal writing, investigating and interviewing, constitutional law, litigation, and the American court systems. The program has an internship requirement that gives students experience and exposure before they begin their career. For example, students can participate in a legal practicum where they work with a practicing attorney to learn how to apply legal concepts in a real-world law practice. Graduates of this program are qualified to work as paralegals in a wide range of legal practices and companies. To be admitted to online programs at Drury University, applicants must provide their high school transcripts or GED certificate.

oedb_description: Founded more than 130 years ago by a Catholic congregation, Duquesne University is rated a top tier university by U.S. News & World Report. The school serves over 9,000 students while maintaining a low 14 to 1 student-to-faculty ratio. Duquesne’s BS in computer systems technology focuses on solving practical technology issues. Courses include information technology for modern organizations, web technologies and applications, and technical communication and writing. Students also take a course in social, ethical, and legal issues in computing technology in order to gain a comprehensive understanding of the field. The school accepts admissions on a rolling basis. New students may begin in the spring or fall terms. Transfer students must have a 2.5 cumulative GPA and grades C or higher. Duquesne accepts 60 credits from previous two-year schools, and up to 90 credits from previous four-year schools. Transfer students must complete the last 30 credits at Duquesne. Duquesne prides itself on providing extensive resources for its online students, including an online learning support center, an online writing center, and an online library. Out-of-state students pay the same tuition as their in-state counterparts.

oedb_description: ECU offers two degree completion online education programs: a BS in middle grades education and a BS in elementary education. Students take either degree on-campus at one of North Carolina’s community colleges to complete their general education courses. This includes on-site, full-time teaching courses. Otherwise, students take major courses entirely online. Both degrees take four years to complete for students who attend full-time. To apply, students fill out an online application that determines whether they will commit to full-time or to part-time study. Depending on their choice, applications are due either in January or in February. Applicants should have a 3.0 GPA at the community college level and must submit ACT or SAT scores. East Carolina University is located in Greenville, North Carolina, and state residents pay discounted tuition.

oedb_description: Eastern Kentucky University in the city of Richmond has a program that offers an online associate degree in paralegal studies . This program teaches students how to understand law and legal practices in the United States. Coursework is completed entirely online, with the exception of 10 credit hours of an in-depth on-campus residency. Coursework covers a wide range of topics, including probate practice, computer applications, criminal law, legal administration, legal research and writing, and litigation. The 64-credit curriculum is made up of 25 hours of general education requirements, 37 hours of core courses, and two hours of elective courses. The residency requirement connects students with practicing attorneys, judges, and paralegals. Students in the residency program also have access to the law library and courthouse vault. To gain admittance to Eastern Kentucky University, applicants must provide their high school transcripts (with a minimum 2.5 GPA) and ACT (with a minimum score of 20) or SAT (a minimum combined math and verbal score of 950) scores. Graduates can pursue employment at insurance companies, law offices and firms, nonprofit organizations, banks, and state and local government agencies.

oedb_description: Founded in 1917, EMU's main campus is in Harrisonburg, Virginia. The university also offers courses at its Lancaster, Pennsylvania, campus and online. EMU's online RN to BSN program is accredited by the Commission on Collegiate Nursing Education and is regarded as one of the best online RN to BSN programs available. The 124-credit program takes 20 months to complete and is ideal for working professionals with tight schedules. The curriculum includes coursework in effective communication, global trends in health, and nursing management. The program culminates in an evidence-based practice project. Applicants must have a minimum 2.0 GPA, must have completed 60 credits of general education coursework, and must submit a writing sample.

oedb_description: Located near Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, Eastern University serves over 3,500 students every year in its traditional and accelerated distance education programs. The online bachelor of science in business administration accepts new students every seven weeks and enables them to earn the degree entirely online or through a hybrid delivery method. The degree plan totals 121 credits and includes major courses in organizational behavior, research methods, and ethical principles of management. Students must complete a capstone project in organizational change, in which they conduct independent research and present their findings and proposed solutions. Tuition for this business administration degree online program is priced at a flat per-credit rate for all students. Distance learners are eligible for financial aid, including institutional scholarships, partner grants, and military benefits. Eastern also provides academic services and professional resources, including personal advising, resume assistance, and networking opportunities, through its Office of Talent and Career Development.

oedb_description: Endicott College offers a BS in psychology that emphasizes research and human behavior. After earning their online degree in psychology, graduates pursue careers in a variety of fields, including law, education, and business. Students examine human behavior from infancy to old age as they learn to assess research and explore methods such as survey design and psychological observation. The 126-credit program offers specializations including early childhood education, homeland security, and Montessori education. Endicott’s four-year internship program helps students gain practical experience while earning their degree. The internship program is likely a main reason that most Endicott students secure employment within one year of completing their bachelor’s degree.

oedb_description: Evangel University offers over 100 academic programs to 2,200 students every year, many of which are accessible to distance learners. The online bachelor of business administration in management teaches students to set business goals and then develop the strategies to successfully meet them. The major curriculum totals 70 credits and includes classes in business ethics, organizational design, and compensation and benefits. Students also learn critical thinking and effective communication skills. This degree prepares graduates for careers in such fields as event management, production design and logistics, and nonprofit administration. To apply for this online business bachelor’s degree program, prospective students need to submit official SAT or ACT scores as well as their official academic transcripts with a minimum GPA of 2.0. The same low online tuition rate is available to all distance learners regardless of residency status.

oedb_description: Franklin Pierce University, located in New Hampshire, offers online associate and bachelor’s degrees as well as certificate programs. The school’s BS in marketing consists of general, liberal arts, core business, and marketing courses. Requirements for this online bachelor's degree in marketing include coursework in electronic commerce and marketing management for a green economy. The university has six academic terms per year, including two summer terms. Online courses are delivered through the Canvas learning management system. Distance learners have access to IT help, a writing center, outreach counseling, and academic and career services. All undergraduate students in the graduate and professional studies college are charged the same per-credit tuition rate. Franklin Pierce also accepts transfer credits and awards credits based on knowledge gained through prior work and life experiences.

oedb_description: Located in Columbus, Ohio, Franklin University designs all of its programs for busy, working adults. As a result, the school offers dozens of flexible online programs. Franklin’s online BS in computer science is language-independent, meaning students’ skills stay relevant even if their primary coding language becomes obsolete. Notable classes for this online computer science degree include human-computer interaction, object-oriented analysis and design, and mobile programming. This degree requires students to complete 124 credits in order to graduate. Franklin accepts a maximum of 94 transfer credits. oedb_description: Located just 35 miles northwest of Pittsburgh in Beaver Falls, Pennsylvania, Geneva College enrolls 1,700 students annually and boasts an average class size of 17. The school’s online bachelor of professional studies in management consists of 36 credits of major coursework in topics like marketing, quantitative business analysis, and principles of management and supervision. Through a curriculum grounded in practical skills development and knowledge of best business practices, students prepare for leadership roles as hospitality managers, real estate brokers, and account executives. Geneva’s online business degree program requires at least 120 credits to complete. The school has a flexible transfer policy, and with the right amount of prior credits, students can graduate in as little as 16 months. Distance learners also have access to Geneva’s financial aid, including loans, scholarships, military discounts, and employer reimbursement.

oedb_description: Mason's bachelor's degree in information technology program is a degree completion program designed for students who have earned an associate degree or completed a significant number of college credits. All courses are available online. However, students without prior college credit may need to take classes at the Fairfax, Virginia, campus. Accredited by the Computing Accreditation Commission of ABET, the IT program requires 120 credits, including the capstone senior design project sequence. Available specializations include health information technology, database technology and programming, and information technology entrepreneurship. The asynchronous format allows students to fit classes around other commitments. The admissions office evaluates all requests for transfer credit and approves up to 90 transfer credits per student.

oedb_description: Situated in the heart of the nation's capital, George Washington University enrolls 26,000 students who study on three campuses, including one located blocks from the White House. Besides world-class academics, the school's ties to federal agencies and several international organizations draw an international student body. Those interested in learning from afar can pursue online doctoral programs in healthcare and engineering. GW's highly ranked school of nursing offers more online doctoral degrees than any other GW unit. Registered nurses receive the training they need to assume roles as advanced practice leaders with several programs such as the 72-credit DNP in adult-gerontology acute care nurse practitioner. Another option, the 43-credit DNP in executive leadership, trains nurse executives with expertise in business, finance, and entrepreneurship. Nursing programs may require clinicals that students complete in northern Virginia, Maryland, or Washington, D.C. Online programs in engineering and occupational therapy round out the portfolio of doctoral-level e-learning options.

oedb_description: Georgia Military College in the city of Milledgeville offers an online associate degree in paralegal studies to students interested in learning about American law, legal research and writing, and legal terminology. Courses help students develop their research, communication, computer, critical thinking, and organizational skills. The curriculum includes a variety of topics, such as law and ethics, family law, legal research and writing, torts and remedies, and alternative dispute resolution. Students in this program learn to understand, prepare, and organize legal documents; develop plans for legal research; evaluate case law, statutory law, and constitutional law; and articulate philosophical principles involving jurisprudence. The program typically takes full-time students two years to complete. Graduates can gain employment as paralegals, legal assistants, or legal secretaries for nonprofit organizations, corporations, private law firms, and government agencies.

oedb_description: Georgia Southern offers a bachelor's of information technology online degree completion program for students with at least two years of college experience: the WebBSIT program. Four public universities in Georgia designed the program to prepare graduates for careers in the growing IT field. The degree requires 126 credits, including coursework in programming, web development, and database design. Available specializations include systems development, web and multimedia, networking and datacenter administration, and information management. Students gain practical experience during internships and a capstone course. Students may take one class each semester or attend classes on a full-time basis to complete the program more quickly. The consortium of schools offering the WebBSIT program allows students to take courses from multiple institutions. Georgia Southern accepts general education transfer credits from any school in the University System of Georgia.

oedb_description: Hampton University’s accelerated BS in criminal justice allows students to complete their bachelor’s degree in a short amount of time. Students in this major take diverse classes such as homeland security, terrorism & national security, and forensics crime scene investigation. The program requires 120 hours to graduate. Students who complete an approved internship receive three credits towards their major. Classes run on an eight-week schedule and students can take up to six credits per cycle. Students do not have to attend class at a specific time, but must adhere to assignment submission dates. Timed exams are taken online and submitted directly to the professor. Most courses do not require students to visit campus.

oedb_description: An accredited online accounting degree is available at Hiram College in Hiram, Ohio. Distance learners can earn a bachelor’s of accounting and financial management 100% online, or they can choose to enroll in a blended program that combines on-campus and elearning. The program prepares graduates to work in entry-level positions in accounting and finance. Some of the available courses include accounting information systems, organizational behavior, and professional responsibilities. To receive the bachelor’s, students must earn 120 credits. By completing additional coursework (30 extra credits, totaling 150), students are eligible to sit for the CPA exam. Another option is to take advantage of one of Hiram’s articulation agreements with Ohio Northern University of Case Western Reserve University and pursue a master’s of accountancy at one of those two institutions. Students finish with a required senior seminar/capstone experience to reflect on key things learned while studying for their degrees.

oedb_description: Hodges University in Naples, Florida offers an associate degree in paralegal studies online that prepares students to work for a variety of law firms. Students in this program learn to conduct legal research, collect evidence, and prepare litigation. The curriculum covers a variety of courses, including civil procedure, legal ethics, legal research and writing, contracts, and criminal law and procedure. Students can enroll and begin taking classes before any semester. Full-time students typically take three courses per term and complete the degree program in about two years. Graduates go on to gain employment at insurance companies; state, local, and federal government agencies; financial institutions; and private law offices. Students also learn about the different specialties available to paralegals, including real estate, immigration, family law, bankruptcy, and intellectual property. To gain admittance to Hodges University, potential students must complete an interview and take a placement test.

oedb_description: Holmes Community College in Goodman, Mississippi offers an associate degree in paralegal studies for individuals interested in working with licensed attorneys as a paralegal or legal assistant. The 61-credit curriculum is comprised of general education requirements and paralegal courses. Students learn the skills and knowledge needed to succeed, including how to interview clients and witnesses, create legal documents, and organize evidence. Course material covers a range of topics, including family law, contracts, law office management, wills and estates, legal writing, and bankruptcy. All of the courses provide abbreviated lectures in the form of podcasts. Upon completion of the program, students earn a technical certificate and can pursue work as paralegals. Graduates of the program can gain employment at nonprofit organizations, private law offices, public institutions, corporations, insurance agencies, financial institutions, or government agencies.

oedb_description: The online bachelor of arts in business administration at Hope International University builds on students’ prior education and experience. In addition to core classes in fundamental areas like international business and organizational ethics, students may also choose to complete a sport business management concentration. At least one internship experience is also required. Distance learners may coordinate with the university to complete an internship in their local community or over the summer through a designated HIU partner. The program prepares graduates for leadership positions in private businesses, nonprofits, and government organizations. HIU supports its students through a number of services, including personal academic coaching, career development, and networking opportunities. Students can complete the online business degree in just three years.

oedb_description: The BS in marketing at Huntington University is offered on campus at two Indiana locations as well as entirely online. The university requires students to complete 128 credits to earn this degree, including 51 credits in marketing, 40 credits in general education, and 37 elective credits. Marketing degree online courses include education in public relations, social media, group behavior, and ethics. Through a business capstone course, marketing students work on a real-world marketing issue and develop a report that demonstrates their learned research, writing, and presentation skills. This capstone also prepares students for the type of work they might encounter in graduate programs. Online courses begin every five weeks and are offered asynchronously. Transfer applicants with more than 24 credits from a regionally-accredited university do not need to submit high school transcripts. All adult students in this program pay the same per-credit tuition. Additionally, more than 90% of Huntington students receive financial assistance through scholarships and other types of aid.

oedb_description: Students enrolled at Hutchinson Community College in Hutchinson, Kansas can pursue a paralegal associate degree online. This 64-credit program includes general education requirements that cover fundamental topics such as word processing, business mathematics, technical writing, microcomputer applications, and small business accounting. The curriculum also teaches paralegal-specific topics, such as litigation, family law, personal injury and civil wrongs, human relations, law and the legal assistant, and legal research and writing. Graduates can find employment in private law firms, public institutions, the government, and corporations. The program ensures students can perform legal research, review and produce legal documents, and meet with company executives. There are also some scholarship opportunities for paralegal students at Hutchinson Community College; an advisor can help individuals identify and apply for these awards.

oedb_description: Indiana Wesleyan University-Marion offers an accounting degree online through its BS in accounting program. Distance learners can complete the entire program online, with no required campus visits. The degree is designed for adults already working in the accounting field and focuses on the core issues of accounting theory, auditing, and taxation. Graduates have the skills needed to enter into or advance in accounting. The degree also provides a solid foundation for individuals interested in taking the CPA exam to be become certified. The degree can be completed in just 18 to 20 months if previous college credits transfer. Elearners benefit from a supportive cohort structure, in which they go through the program and take classes with the same group of students. Tuition is the same cost regardless of where individuals live.

oedb_description: The business school at Johns Hopkins University offers a flexible, part-time online MBA program designed for working professionals; the average student in this program has 9.4 years of full-time work experience. Online learners pursuing this MBA online degree can customize the program to fit their individuals needs by choosing to complete the program entirely online or in a hybrid format. However, some business communication, negotiation, and leadership in organizations courses require in-person residencies at the Baltimore campus for one to three days. Courses are offered in eight-week terms, mostly asynchronously, with a few required synchronous real-time activities. Students can opt for specializations in financial business, healthcare management, or leading organizations. Consisting of 54 credits, most students complete this program in 2.5 years. With a large network of alumni and highly respected professionals, graduates of this program gain connections in multiple industries including financial services, the government, technology, and healthcare.

oedb_description: Located in Elgin, Illinois, Judson University offers a bachelor’s in applied psychology that covers the theories and methodologies used to address human behavioral problems. This online accredited psychology degree prepares students for careers managing others in a variety of fields. Course requirements include statistics, psychological measurement, lifespan development, and faith issues. The 46 required core credits develop problem-solving skills, explore how social identify influences values and beliefs, and improve spoken and written communication skills. Judson students undertake research projects, during which they assess and disseminate findings. Students also complete a supervised internship at an organization of their choice.

oedb_description: Kansas City Kansas Community College offers students a paralegal online associate degree that teaches learners how to perform detailed legal work under the supervision of a licensed attorney. This program can be completed entirely online, except for an internship requirement; however, the college also offers on-campus and hybrid options to students. Distance learners must complete 69 credits to graduate from the program, including 25 general education credits and 44 technical credits. The curriculum covers a range of topics, including criminal law for the paralegal, litigation, legal interviewing and investigation, and microcomputer business software. The internship required for graduation provides students with exposure to their field and gives them hands-on experience. Students interested in getting more experience in the form of administrative work also have access to career training at the school’s technical education center.

oedb_description: Keiser University, located in Ft. Lauderdale, Florida, offers an online paralegal associate degree that teaches students about legal practices in the United States. To earn an associate of arts in paralegal studies, students complete a 60-credit curriculum. The program helps develop writing and speaking, analytical, and technical skills. Coursework covers a variety of topics including legal research and writing, real property, family law, civil litigation, criminal law, torts law, contracts, and wills, trusts, and estates. Coursework is divided into two sections: 36 credit hours of general education courses and 24 credit hours of paralegal courses. The program prepares students to work in law firms, businesses, corporations, civil and criminal practices, and for lawyers as a paralegal. Prospective students can opt to meet with an admissions advisor in person and make a campus visit.

oedb_description: Located outside Atlanta, KSU is one of the top undergraduate engineering schools offering online programs. KSU's standout engineering program is the bachelor's of science in industrial engineering technology , which is offered on-campus and online. Professors deliver face-to-face lectures to students in the on-campus program, and the lectures are streamed live and recorded for online students. This allows for both synchronous and asynchronous learning; distance learners may participate in classes real-time or view lectures when their schedule allows. The program has a managerial focus; students study the management of human resources, materials, and capital in business and industrial settings. Graduates are prepared to work in government, business, and industry environments, in fields such as supply chain management and quality control.

oedb_description: Located in Bristol, Tennessee, King University offers a bachelor's of science in criminal justice . The degree can open doors to careers in child and family law, state and federal law enforcement, and in the prison and probate systems. King offers a course on restorative justice that focuses on repairing and restoring relationships between victims and criminals. Students must complete 48 credits in the major with a total of 124 credits to graduate. Students can complete the degree in as few as 16 months. The online program has specific start and end dates and is not self-paced. To apply for the major, students must have 45 hours of prior college coursework completed. All students must pass a general education exam before graduating from King and receiving a diploma.

oedb_description: Located in Plymouth, Wisconsin, LU serves nearly 3,500 students and is affiliated with the United Church of Christ. The university offers a bachelor’s in psychology that builds students' understanding of scientific inquiry methods and strengthens their ability to observe and articulate the role of the brain in behavior, thought, and emotion. Coursework examines the social, biological, and cognitive capabilities of organisms as they adapt to their environments. This accredited online psychology degree requires 48 major credits and 24 credits in areas such as brain, body, and mind. Students can participate in an internship to fulfill elective requirements, during which they apply classroom studies under the guidance of a site supervisor and a faculty member. Graduates pursue further education and enter careers in areas such as counseling and social work.

oedb_description: Lake Superior College in Duluth, Minnesota has an online paralegal associate degree . Graduates of this program are prepared to work as paralegals or legal assistants, preparing documents, collecting evidence, and conducting legal research for licensed attorneys. Students learn about proper legal terminology, the American legal system, methods for conducting interviews and investigations with clients and witnesses, state and federal court procedures and rules, and ethical obligations for attorneys and paralegals. The program is comprised of 60 credits, which cover a variety of topics including business communications, civil litigation, legal document processing, and computers in the law office. The program also has a paralegal capstone component, which requires students to put together a presentation to demonstrate their learned skills. This program is typically completed in two years. Graduates are prepared for careers as judicial assistants, probate paralegals, legal assistants, and real estate paralegals.

oedb_description: Students attending Laramie County Community College in Cheyenne, Wyoming can pursue an associate degree in paralegal studies online. This program teaches distance learners how to perform legal research and writing, interview clients and witnesses, and utilize the computer for research. Students develop their technical, analytical, communication, and critical thinking skills as they complete coursework. The 64-credit curriculum encompasses a number of topics, including law office management, business law, family law, real estate and property law, computer applications in the law, and probate practices and procedures. The paralegal program also requires students to complete a paralegal internship. This internship provides students with real-world experience in their chosen field, allowing them to see first-hand how the skills they learn at school are used by paralegals.

oedb_description: Located in Longview, Texas, LeTourneau University offers two Christ-centered online degrees in education: the BA in interdisciplinary studies with a teaching certificate in early childhood-6th grade and the BA in interdisciplinary studies with certification for 4-8th grade . Both programs consist of 120 credits that include general core, major, and cognate courses. Both programs require students to complete a 12-credit sequence in theology, along with nine elective credits, with each requiring six credits of student teaching. Students take classes in five-week-long accelerated terms, and certificates may be paired with optional minors in leadership, management, project management, psychology, and reading. Students can enroll during one of nine dates per year, and applicants must submit proof of high school graduation or a GED, transcripts, and ACT or SAT scores. Transfer applicants with fewer than 15 college credits must submit standardized test scores. In-state and out-of-state online students pay the same tuition rate.

oedb_description: Located in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, Lehigh University's college of business and economics offers a one-year, full-time, flexible online MBA designed for experienced professionals. This 36-credit online MBA degree begins with a mandatory, on-campus weekend orientation. At the end of the program, students complete a three-credit final capstone project, in which they participate in case discussions and a simulation. Prerequisites for the program include financial accounting, statistics, and principles of economics. Lehigh MBA students typically have an average GPA of 3.4 and eight years of work experience. Applicants must have at least two years of work experience; however, this does not necessarily have to be in a business-related function. GMAT/GRE scores may be waived if applicants have 10 years of full-time professional experience or a master’s degree with six years of full-time professional experience.

oedb_description: A Cambridge, Massachusetts, institution with a history of training educators, Lesley University offers an online bachelor’s in psychology for students interested in human behavior, brain functions, and social processes. As one of the country’s best online psychology degree programs, the bachelor's prepares graduates for careers in fields such as mental health and business. Courses cover topics such as personality, memory, individual development, and culture. Students explore psychological theories and approaches. In addition to coursework, students complete a supervised internship, during which they gain hands-on experience. While the program is open to most U.S. students, residents of Georgia, Kentucky, Oregon, Washington, and Nevada are ineligible to apply.

oedb_description: With 97% of its students receiving financial aid, Lewis University strives to make education affordable for all learners. Although it is a Catholic university that teaches Lasallian values, Lewis serves students of all faiths and backgrounds. The school’s accelerated online bachelor’s degree in computer science allows students to graduate with their degree in a short amount of time. Students may choose between a bachelor of arts or a bachelor of science. Topics include programming languages, cybersecurity, and artificial intelligence. The eight-week online schedule allows students to complete their studies faster than usual. Only transfer students qualify for this degree option. Applicants must possess at least 30 transferable credit hours and hold a cumulative GPA of 2.0 or higher. Students with prior programming and coding experience may earn credit for prior learning. In-state and out-of-state students pay the same tuition rate.

oedb_description: Liberty University in Lynchburg, Virginia offers an associate degree in paralegal studies that can be completed entirely online. The program teaches students the foundations of law and is comprised of 60 credits. Students take coursework related to communication; math, science, and technology; information literacy; critical thinking; Christian life and thought; and paralegal studies. Distance learners can take up to three credits of electives in addition to their required coursework. Paralegal courses cover topics that include legal research, legal writing, criminal practice and procedures, civil practice, and foundations of law. Graduates of this program can go on to work as paralegals, legal assistants, legal secretaries, and government workers. In order to gain admittance, applicants must have a minimum 2.0 GPA and submit their high school diploma or GED certificate. Students in the program are able to transfer 45 credits from another institution, but at least 15 credits must be completed at Liberty University to graduate with an associate degree in paralegal studies.

oedb_description: Loyola offers a convenient path to degree completion with its bachelor's of information technology online degree. Students may complete all classes online or take some classes on the Chicago campus. The degree completion program comprises 13 core classes, which provide a foundation in computer programming, web development, and network management. Loyola prepares online students for success by pairing students with faculty who specialize in online instruction. The accelerated format allows students to begin coursework at five start dates throughout the year and to complete classes in as few as eight weeks. The curriculum includes hands-on learning opportunities such as the capstone service-learning project. The university waives two courses for students who plan to continue their education with a master's degree in information technology from Loyola.

oedb_description: Madisonville Community College in Kentucky offers students an associate degree in paralegal studies , preparing individuals for entry-level paralegal careers at corporations, law firms, courts, and government agencies. The curriculum is comprised of 66 credits, including 21 credits of general education requirements, nine credits of technical support courses, and 36 credits of paralegal courses. The program is typically completed in two years by full-time students. Graduates can find employment as paralegals in a variety of settings. Additionally, students interested in continuing their education beyond an associate degree can transfer their degree and completed credits to a number of universities. The career services center at the college is available to distance learners and offers job placement assistance. The paralegal studies program is fully online and completed through the Blackboard learning management system. To successfully graduate, students must have a minimum GPA of 2.0.

oedb_description: Malone University is located in Canton, Ohio. However, students across the country can take advantage of the school’s BA in organizational management and marketing management , which is an online accelerated degree program. The organizational management major requires 36 credit hours, and marketing management is a secondary major that requires four additional marketing courses. Through this adult degree-completion program, students can complete their online marketing degree in as little as 12 months, and every student creates a personalized course of study to guide them to graduation. The program is delivered asynchronously through the Moodle learning management system, which offers 24/7 technology assistance. This adult degree-completion program boasts an 80% graduation rate. Students are also encouraged to use the Malone Management Mentor Network (M3N), which provides distance learners with advice and encouragement. Online organizational management students are charged the same per-credit rate as on-campus students, and financial aid and payment plans are available.

oedb_description: Founded in 1872 in St. Louis, Missouri, Maryville offers a variety of fully online programs, including one of the most respected online RN to BSN programs in the state. Maryville’s RN to BSN program is accredited by the Commission on Collegiate Nursing Education and approved by the Missouri State Board of Nursing. The 128-credit program instructs students in topics such as psychosocial nursing care, nursing care of children, and pathophysiology for nursing practice. The curriculum culminates in a capstone project on public health. Online courses are delivered through Canvas and utilize synchronous and asynchronous learning methods. Applicants must have at least a 2.5 GPA, and applicants for whom English is a second language must have a TOEFL score of 85.
